title: hieracium murorum subsp. umbrosum
intro: Hieracium murorum subsp. umbrosum, also known as Wall Hawkweed, is a perennial plant belonging to the dandelion family that is native to Europe and western Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe digestive issues and reduce inflammation in the body. The plant's leaves and roots have been used to treat a variety of health issues, including arthritis, fever, and respiratory problems, and it has also been used as an antiseptic to aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Hieracium murorum subsp. umbrosum can be consumed as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and it is also used topically as a poultice or salve to treat skin conditions and injuries.
